description: Real-time crypto betting engine — Java 21 / Spring Boot. Users bet on crypto price direction, bets auto-settle. TDD approach: tests first, then implementation.

userStories.title: Spring Boot project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a properly configured Spring Boot project with all dependencies so I can start building features.
acceptanceCriteria: Spring Boot 3.x project with Java 21, Gradle Kotlin DSL
Dependencies: spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, spring-boot-starter-websocket, spring-boot-starter-data-redis, flyway-core, postgresql driver, spring-boot-starter-actuator
Test dependencies: spring-boot-starter-test, h2 database, embedded-redis (com.github.codemonstur:embedded-redis or similar)
application.yml: local profile with postgres (localhost:5432), redis (localhost:6379), flyway enabled, ddl-auto=validate
application-test.yml: H2 in-memory DB, embedded Redis config
application-prod.yml: env var placeholders for DB_URL, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, REDIS_URL
Virtual Threads enabled: spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true
Health endpoint enabled: /actuator/health
Base package: com.cryptobet.engine
Project compiles: ./gradlew build succeeds
Application context loads in test: @SpringBootTest test passes
userStories.notes: No TDD — pure scaffolding. Verify by compiling and context load test.

userStories.title: Wallet entity and Flyway migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Wallet entity and database table so wallet operations can be built on top.
acceptanceCriteria: Wallet entity in com.cryptobet.engine.model: id (UUID, generated), balance (BigDecimal), currency (String, default 'USD'), createdAt (Instant), updatedAt (Instant)

Flyway migration V1__create_wallets_table.sql: id UUID PRIMARY KEY, balance DECIMAL(19,4) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, currency VARCHAR(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD', created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
WalletRepository extends JpaRepository<Wallet, UUID>
Test: Flyway migration runs successfully on H2
Test: Wallet entity can be saved and retrieved with correct field types
Test: balance stored as BigDecimal, not double
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: write entity persistence tests first, verify they fail, then create entity + migration.

userStories.title: Wallet REST API — create and get
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a wallet and check my balance.
acceptanceCriteria: WalletController in com.cryptobet.engine.controller
WalletService in com.cryptobet.engine.service
POST /api/wallets with body {"initialBalance": 1000.00} returns 201 with {"id": "uuid", "balance": 1000.00, "currency": "USD"}
GET /api/wallets/{id} returns 200 with wallet details
GET /api/wallets/{nonexistent-id} returns 404
Test (write first): POST creates wallet and returns 201 with UUID and balance
Test (write first): GET returns correct wallet data
Test (write first): GET with non-existent UUID returns 404
Request/response DTOs — no entity exposed directly in API
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: write controller integration tests with MockMvc first, verify they fail, then implement controller + service.

userStories.title: Wallet deposit with pessimistic locking
userStories.description: As a user, I want to deposit funds into my wallet safely under concurrent access.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/wallets/{id}/deposit with body {"amount": 500.00} returns 200 with updated wallet
Pessimistic locking: @Lock(LockModeType.PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) on wallet query used during deposit
@Transactional on deposit operation
Test (write first): deposit increases wallet balance
Test (write first): deposit with zero amount returns 400
Test (write first): deposit with negative amount returns 400
Test (write first): deposit to non-existent wallet returns 404
Test (write first): concurrent deposits (5 threads depositing $100 each) result in exactly $500 increase — no lost updates
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: write tests including concurrency test first. The concurrent test is critical — use ExecutorService with CountDownLatch.

userStories.title: Odds service with vigorish
userStories.description: As the house, I need an odds service that calculates decimal odds with configurable margin.
acceptanceCriteria: OddsService in com.cryptobet.engine.service
OddsConfig in com.cryptobet.engine.config — reads margin from application.yml (betting.margin, default 0.08)
getOdds() returns BigDecimal 1.85 with default 8% margin (formula: 2.0 / (1 + margin), scale 2, HALF_UP)
calculatePayout(stake, odds) returns stake * odds with BigDecimal, scale 4, HALF_UP
Test (write first): default odds = 1.85
Test (write first): payout for $100 at 1.85 = $185.00
Test (write first): payout for $50 at 1.85 = $92.50
Test (write first): changing margin to 0.10 gives odds of 1.82 (2.0 / 1.10)
Test (write first): all results are BigDecimal, never double
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: pure unit tests on OddsService. No DB or Redis needed.

userStories.title: Bet entity and Flyway migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Bet entity and database table so bet operations can be built on top.
acceptanceCriteria: BetDirection enum: UP, DOWN
BetStatus enum: PLACED, ACTIVE, WON, LOST, VOID, PUSH

Bet entity in com.cryptobet.engine.model: id (UUID), walletId (UUID), symbol (String), direction (BetDirection), stake (BigDecimal), odds (BigDecimal), status (BetStatus), priceAtPlacement (BigDecimal), priceAtResolution (BigDecimal nullable), placedAt (Instant), resolveAt (Instant), resolvedAt (Instant nullable), idempotencyKey (String unique)
Flyway migration V2__create_bets_table.sql with proper column types, indexes on wallet_id, status, resolve_at, UNIQUE on idempotency_key
BetRepository extends JpaRepository<Bet, UUID> with findByWalletId and findByStatusAndResolveAtBefore methods
Test: migration runs on H2
Test: Bet entity persists and retrieves with all fields correct
Test: idempotency_key unique constraint prevents duplicate
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: write persistence tests first, including unique constraint test.

userStories.title: Bet placement API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to place a bet that atomically deducts stake from my wallet and creates the bet record.
acceptanceCriteria: BetService in com.cryptobet.engine.service
BetController: POST /api/bets with body {"walletId": "uuid", "symbol": "BTCUSDT", "direction": "UP", "stake": 50.00, "durationSeconds": 60, "idempotencyKey": "unique-key"}
Returns 201 with bet details including locked odds (1.85), status ACTIVE, resolveAt
Atomic: wallet balance deducted + bet created in single @Transactional
Wallet locked with pessimistic write lock during placement
priceAtPlacement set to a placeholder value for now (0 — real prices come in US-012)
Test (write first): place bet returns 201, wallet balance decreased by stake
Test (write first): insufficient balance returns 409
Test (write first): stake < 1 returns 400
Test (write first): stake > 10000 returns 400
Test (write first): invalid duration (not 30/60/120/300) returns 400
Test (write first): duplicate idempotency_key returns 200 with original bet, wallet not double-charged
Test (write first): concurrent bets on same wallet — total deducted equals sum of stakes, no double-spend
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: write all controller integration tests first. Concurrent test uses ExecutorService + CountDownLatch. Use OddsService for odds.

userStories.title: Exposure tracking via Redis — Lua script and service
userStories.description: As the house, I need to track and limit exposure per symbol+direction in Redis atomically.
acceptanceCriteria: ExposureService in com.cryptobet.engine.service
ExposureConfig: betting.maxLiabilityPerSymbol default 10000000 (cents = $100,000)
Redis key pattern: exposure:{symbol}:{direction} (e.g. exposure:BTCUSDT:UP)
All amounts stored as cents (long) — multiply BigDecimal by 100, convert to long
Lua script for atomic check-and-increment: reads current, checks limit, increments if under, returns 1 (accepted) or 0 (rejected)
decrementExposure() method for settlement
Test (write first): incrementExposure adds correct cents value to Redis key
Test (write first): exposure at limit — next bet rejected (returns false)
Test (write first): exposure under limit — bet accepted (returns true)
Test (write first): decrementExposure reduces value
Test (write first): concurrent increments don't exceed limit (use embedded Redis in test)
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: use embedded Redis for tests. Lua script loaded from classpath resource file.

userStories.title: Integrate exposure check into bet placement
userStories.description: As the house, I need bet placement to check exposure limits before accepting a bet.
acceptanceCriteria: BetService checks ExposureService.incrementExposure() before creating bet
If exposure would exceed limit: reject bet with 409 and message 'Exposure limit exceeded'
Exposure incremented only after bet successfully persisted (or within same logical operation)
Test (write first): bet rejected when exposure limit would be exceeded
Test (write first): bet accepted when under limit, exposure incremented
Test (write first): rejected bet does not increment exposure
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: integration test with embedded Redis. May need to mock or set low exposure limit for testing.

userStories.title: Global error handling and validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need consistent error responses across all endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: GlobalExceptionHandler with @ControllerAdvice in com.cryptobet.engine.controller
Error response DTO: {"error": "CODE", "message": "human readable", "details": {}}
Custom exceptions: InsufficientBalanceException, ExposureLimitExceededException, WalletNotFoundException, BetNotFoundException
MethodArgumentNotValidException -> 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with field details
WalletNotFoundException / BetNotFoundException -> 404 NOT_FOUND
InsufficientBalanceException -> 409 INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE
ExposureLimitExceededException -> 409 EXPOSURE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
Generic Exception -> 500 INTERNAL_ERROR (no stack trace in response)
Bean Validation on all request DTOs: @NotNull, @Positive, @NotBlank etc.
Test (write first): validation error returns 400 with correct format
Test (write first): not found returns 404 with correct format
Test (write first): insufficient balance returns 409 with correct format
Test (write first): unknown exception returns 500 without stack trace
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: test error format first. Refactor existing controllers to use custom exceptions if needed.

userStories.title: Binance WebSocket price feed — client and cache
userStories.description: As the system, I need to consume live crypto prices from Binance and cache them in memory.
acceptanceCriteria: PriceSnapshot record/class: symbol (String), price (BigDecimal), timestamp (Instant)
PriceCacheService with ConcurrentHashMap<String, PriceSnapshot>. Methods: getPrice(symbol), updatePrice(symbol, snapshot), getAllPrices()
BinancePriceMessage — DTO for parsing Binance JSON: {"s": "BTCUSDT", "p": "64230.50", "T": 1234567890}
BinanceWebSocketClient connects to wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws, subscribes to trade streams
Configurable symbols in application.yml: betting.symbols (default: BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT)
Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s
Force reconnect if no message in 30s
Test (write first): BinancePriceMessage parsed correctly from JSON string
Test (write first): PriceCacheService.updatePrice stores and retrieves snapshot
Test (write first): stale price (older timestamp) does not overwrite newer price
Test (write first): getAllPrices returns all cached symbols
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: unit test parsing and cache logic. WebSocket client itself tested via connection logging — hard to mock Binance in fast tests. Focus tests on the pure logic parts.

userStories.title: Wire real prices into bet placement
userStories.description: As the system, I need bet placement to record the actual price at placement time from the live price cache.
acceptanceCriteria: BetService reads current price from PriceCacheService when placing bet
priceAtPlacement set to current cached price for the bet's symbol
If no price available for symbol (cache miss), reject bet with 400: 'Price not available for symbol'
Test (write first): bet placement records current price from cache
Test (write first): bet placement with unknown symbol (no cached price) returns 400
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: mock PriceCacheService in test to provide deterministic prices.

userStories.title: Settlement scheduler — core logic
userStories.description: As the system, I need to automatically resolve expired bets by comparing prices.
acceptanceCriteria: SettlementService in com.cryptobet.engine.service
@Scheduled method runs every 1 second (fixedRate = 1000)
Finds ACTIVE bets where resolveAt <= Instant.now(), LIMIT 100
For each bet: get current price from PriceCacheService
Direction UP + current > placement price -> WON
Direction UP + current < placement price -> LOST
Direction DOWN + current < placement price -> WON
Direction DOWN + current > placement price -> LOST
Current == placement price -> PUSH
WON: credit wallet with stake * odds in same transaction, set status WON
LOST: set status LOST (no wallet change, stake already deducted)
PUSH: refund stake to wallet in same transaction, set status PUSH
Set priceAtResolution and resolvedAt on bet
Decrement Redis exposure after settlement
Each bet settled in its own @Transactional (one failure doesn't block others)
Test (write first): WON scenario — wallet balance increases by stake * odds
Test (write first): LOST scenario — wallet balance unchanged
Test (write first): PUSH scenario — wallet balance increases by stake (refund)
Test (write first): bet fields updated: status, priceAtResolution, resolvedAt
Test (write first): only ACTIVE bets with resolveAt <= now are picked up
Test (write first): exposure decremented after settlement
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: mock PriceCacheService to control prices. Use embedded Redis for exposure tests. Disable @Scheduled in test — call settle method directly.

userStories.title: WebSocket price push to clients
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive live crypto prices via WebSocket.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket endpoint at /ws/prices using Spring WebSocketHandler (raw, no STOMP)
WebSocketConfig registers handler at /ws/prices
Client sends: {"type":"subscribe","symbols":["BTCUSDT","ETHUSDT"]}
Server pushes: {"type":"price","symbol":"BTCUSDT","price":"64230.50","timestamp":1234567890}
PriceWebSocketHandler manages sessions and subscriptions in ConcurrentHashMap
Only push updates for symbols client subscribed to
Throttle: max 1 update per symbol per second per client
Remove session on disconnect
Test (write first): subscribe message registers client for correct symbols
Test (write first): price update sent only for subscribed symbols
Test (write first): disconnect removes session
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: test handler logic in isolation. Use Spring's WebSocket test support or test handler methods directly.

userStories.title: Bet status and history API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view my bet history and individual bet details.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/wallets/{walletId}/bets — paginated list sorted by placedAt desc
Optional query param: status (filter by BetStatus)
Pagination: page (default 0) and size (default 20) query params
GET /api/bets/{id} — single bet with all fields + potentialPayout (stake * odds)
Response DTO includes potentialPayout calculated field
Test (write first): list bets returns paginated results
Test (write first): filter by status returns only matching
Test (write first): get single bet includes potentialPayout
Test (write first): empty wallet returns empty list, not 404
Test (write first): non-existent bet returns 404
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: seed test data, assert pagination and filtering.

userStories.title: WebSocket bet updates
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive real-time notifications when my bets settle.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket endpoint at /ws/bets
Client subscribes: {"type":"subscribe","walletId":"uuid"}
Server pushes on settlement: {"type":"bet_update","betId":"uuid","status":"WON","payout":"185.00"}
Only push to clients subscribed to the bet's walletId
Push on: WON, LOST, PUSH, VOID
BetWebSocketHandler manages sessions by walletId
SettlementService calls BetWebSocketHandler after settling each bet
Test (write first): subscribe registers client for wallet updates
Test (write first): settlement triggers push to correct client
Test (write first): client for different wallet does NOT receive update
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: TDD: test handler logic. Wire into SettlementService.

userStories.title: Production config — Dockerfile and docker-compose
userStories.description: As a developer, I need production-ready Docker setup for deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile: multi-stage build — stage 1 gradle build, stage 2 slim JRE (eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine)
docker-compose.yml with services: app, postgres (16-alpine), redis (7-alpine)
App service reads DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL from environment
Postgres service with health check, persistent volume
Redis service with health check
App depends_on postgres and redis with condition: service_healthy
docker-compose build succeeds
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: No TDD — infrastructure. Verify by building Docker image.

userStories.title: Production config — CI pipeline and structured logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need GitHub Actions CI and production logging.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml: checkout, setup Java 21, gradle build, gradle test, docker build
Structured JSON logging for prod profile: add logback-spring.xml with JSON encoder for prod, plain text for local
application-prod.yml: logging config pointer to logback-spring.xml
CI runs on push to main and pull requests
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: No TDD — CI/infra. Verify CI yaml is valid and logging config is correct.
